did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a correction to a study titled 'Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images.' The original study utilized a convolutional neural network (a deep learning method) for the dermatology task of detecting acral melanoma using dermoscopy images.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The document is a correction to a 2018 study titled 'Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images' (PLoS ONE 13(3): e0193321). The original study is an original experimental/technical study involving a convolutional neural network for dermatology. The correction itself is a formal amendment to that research paper, not a review or survey of literature.

title: Correction: Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images
brief_summary: This document is a formal correction to a 2018 research paper titled 'Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images.' The original study utilized a convolutional neural network to detect acral melanoma from dermoscopy images. The correction addresses administrative errors, specifically correcting the affiliations for authors Sejung Yang and Sang Wook Lee. It also updates the Data Availability statement to indicate that data are available within the paper, its Supporting Information files, or via figshare. The paper does not report new clinical results or evaluation metrics, as it serves only to amend the original publication's metadata and data access information.

lesion_types_studied.details: The paper is a correction to a study titled 'Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images,' indicating that acral melanoma was the primary target class for detection in the original research.

all_task_types.target: Acral melanoma detection (binary classification of dermoscopy images as melanoma or non-melanoma)
all_task_types.details: The paper is a correction to a study titled 'Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images.' The original study utilized a convolutional neural network for the task of detecting acral melanoma, which implies a classification task (distinguishing melanoma from non-melanoma). The correction itself does not report new metrics but confirms the nature of the original work.

datasets_used.name: Dermoscopy image dataset (via figshare)
datasets_used.description: The dataset used in the original study 'Acral melanoma detection using a convolutional neural network for dermoscopy images' (PLoS ONE 13(3): e0193321), which is made available within the paper, its Supporting Information files, or via figshare at https://figshare.com/s/a8c22c09f999f60a81bd.

use.training: Used for training the convolutional neural network in the original study.
use.validation: Used for validation in the original study.
use.testing: Used for testing in the original study.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: No hypothesis testing was reported; this document is a correction to a previous study addressing administrative errors and data availability.

results_obtained_in_each_dataset.note: The provided document is a correction to a previous study (Yu et al., 2018) and does not report new quantitative results, metrics, or evaluation data. It only corrects author affiliations and the Data Availability statement. Therefore, no specific dataset results are contained within this text.

conclusions: The paper is a formal correction to a previous study and does not report new scientific conclusions, clinical performance metrics, or technical evaluations regarding the AI method.
The primary administrative conclusion is the correction of author affiliations: Sang Wook Lee is affiliated with Sogang University, and Sejung Yang is affiliated with Yonsei University.
The Data Availability statement was corrected to indicate that data are available within the paper, its Supporting Information files, or via figshare.
important_remarks: This document is a formal correction to a previous research paper (Yu et al., 2018) and does not report new clinical results, evaluation metrics, or technical evaluations.
The correction addresses administrative errors, specifically correcting the affiliations for authors Sejung Yang and Sang Wook Lee.
The Data Availability statement was updated to indicate that data are available within the paper, its Supporting Information files, or via figshare.
No new statistical methods, hypothesis testing, or performance metrics are reported in this correction.
